HealthScore, and we get a little bit more granular, but if there are anything that has a direct stitch to SEO performance that, you know, you guys are able to catch, being able to put that ahead of us, like, early and often, still super helpful.
We'll just, you know, plan to kind of execute internally.

Sure, yeah, like, we can drop, like, when it comes to audits, like, audits are not something that you do, like, every quarter, but we can definitely, like, check your site health every month, and then if there's a need for an additional audit, then, you know, like, we could talk about it, like, you know, in a couple of months, like, zooming more into, like, six plus.
But, yeah, thanks for the mark.

I can always, like, update that.

And, basically, like, you can see the, you know, the overall performance, like, sessions and engaged sessions, the sessions by referral, so basically, like, organic, and query distribution.

Google Search Console is the second one.

I think, like, cool.
Yep.
Sorry, just to jump in.

I'm not 100% sure if this is still the case, because I'm doing less reporting on the one of the metrics I was looking at previously, but I believe with the events, I want to say it was with, like, the key events, that we needed to do more of a manual grab.
And I'm not sure if that is still the case.
And maybe that's something we can look on to.
And yeah, maybe once I've seen, once I've had a chance to dig into this, then I can kind of let you know what I have and that it matches up.
Because I think something was kind of like over, it just wasn't pulling it correctly, basically.

It's also not lost on our CEO that brand and awareness is...
A great function for us, especially when it comes to resulting in searches.

I think as we start to kind of map out what these funnels look like, how we want traffic to flow through our site, and what type of function does a first-time visit in, you know, category or cluster A versus B, what are expectations of that kind of next step is going to be important for us to measure.
So to Kenzie's point, I don't know if these, like, events are accurate at all.

Here, I have a question about Google Analytics 4, because you, Tyler, mentioned that you would like to have groups by the product, et cetera, so are you planning to update your analytics tracking plan so you have it in your Google Analytics so we could pull this data into reports, or how are you planning to measure it going forward?

Yeah, so it's all going to kind of flow or trickle down from that heat map that we're putting together, heat map to funnel to scorecard, and then that will inform what we want to track from a scorecard standpoint, because right now, I think we've done a lot of reporting of like,
Diligent, excluding MDL, MDL, board effects, you know, different kind of like lots of data.

But like thinking through what are the one to five to 10, and I don't know how many different entry points there will be on DLC, but there's going to be different core problems, commonalities.
it's like kind of laddering experimentation.
So like if anyone's been in ethnography, going outside of a gym and say like, why are you going to the gym?
Because I want to work out.
Well, why, why, why, why, why, why?
Right.
And we can kind of boil that down to a couple of these terminal entry points for us.
And then from there, be a little bit more predictive and, and also like set up the right mechanisms to get people from point A to point B or point Z, whatever that is.
Which is demo request, pricing request for us.
So I think looking at it through that kind of lens is going to be helpful for us to start to kind of break down these content clusters.
And then as we add new content or new refreshes on that, we can measure the direct impact of those efforts down through the funnel, even if they're not showing up in HMCLs.
So being able to like create a way to communicate that value up to our leadership team is going to be important for me because there's a ton of lost value when we're only measuring against HMCLs.
And so we have some good internal conversations with RCML and figuring out, you know, how do we ensure that there isn't any lost value in what we're pushing?
Cool.
Totally makes sense.
And yeah, waiting for the heat map so we can like basically merge these two viewpoints of like our view when it comes to analytics plus cluster.

And we filtered out also non-growthx URLs to basically like separate these two into these two buckets.
And I think like that's, you know, like this type of data complemented with what Tyler said about like heat maps and top fun, like basically like user path.
I would say like top funnel, bottom panel and mid funnel tagging, plus the clusters, we will have like a cloud.
What is driving engagement?
Your website is basically huge.
So we have to focus on the content that is driving the most signups and further drives MQLs that turn into SQLs and customers.
So we would like to be very focused with this type of engagement.
And yeah, like the last reports, think like basically more like there are like landing page reports.
So again, like Flick's impressions, CTR.
So this is like Google Search Console data basically in one pub and queries as well, data from Google Search Console.
But what I wanted to show you, I think, and it's the most interesting is basically like referral breakdown by LLM.
So what we already also discussed with Tyler and you can see here, you know, like how much.

much of the organic traffic you're getting per LLM, which gives us a good idea of what is the visibility, and this is paired with scrunched data around specific prompts that we are tracking, so we have a full picture of your LLM presence together.
Are we able to bring in impressions, position, that type of stuff?
So this is Google Analytics data, which means Google Search Console measures impressions, but only when it comes to Google, right?
So Google provides this data, and LLMs don't provide this impression data, so there's no way to get this data, and this is for all the brands in the world.
Unfortunately, LLMs are not really sharing this data, so this is a limitation that's outside of our control.
What about, like, what Scrunch is measuring, which is just percentage against, like, our competitor?
Is there any way to bring kind of that into this?
So, Scrunch measures, like, impressions in a way, because, like, for, like, a given prompt, you have information whether you appeared as a brand for this prompt or not.
So we can, like, say it's an impression, right?
So this way, like, we can measure it, but I would just call it, like, presence, as Scrunch calls it.
Yeah, think even just, like, relative values can be helpful as long as we're clear that these are not absolutes.
Like, a lot of our data are estimative as well when we're pulling competitive sets.
So I think if the way that Scrunch reports on it, like, a percentage of, an estimated percentage of, and just being able to, like, measure are we losing positioning or are we improving?

Positioning, not just in terms of where Diligent is mentioned across these prompts that we're tracking, but positioning just across the broader landscape of searches, how we measure against our competitors.

Yeah, so, like, here you can see, like, this, like, breakdown, so competitive presence, and I think, like, as Aida was, like, sending you these reports weekly, so we'll continue sending you these dashboard screenshots, basically, so we'll have, like, full data set, you know, week by week about your competitive presence here.
Basically, you know, last 90 days is, like, 38%, basically.
Okay.
And I think, Tyler, you were also asking if it would be possible to bring scrunch data into Looker, right?
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, this is something that's kind of been an ongoing discussion internally.
I can check with Dave and...
And the team to see if there's been any progress on that.
The thing with Scrunch is that it's just very, very new.
So their capabilities are pretty rudimentary when you actually look at the features.
But I'll ask and get back to you if this is going to be a possibility in the near future.
It all just kind of rolls up to a theme of like, are we owning our data and reporting?
And, you know, it's not, we all understand that this is very like early on in monitoring and like every single tool is going to have its limitations.

But I know tools like we did like a two-day trial run of Ahrefs, like LMM monitoring, and it was able to give like estimated impressions, not in the breakdown of like how we are able to like categorize things within Scrunch.
So it's like, yeah, I think just every platform is going to have a little bit different of a unique way of approaching LLM monitoring, as long as we have like.
A consistent baseline for a period of time, consistency, I think matters more so than like absolute impressions versus, you know, percentage of, you know, relative positioning.
Just because like we still as an organization need to figure out how we contextualize this information and how the relationship between this and traditional search is shifting.
So it's like, if we don't have a consistent way of contextualizing it, if we don't have consistent data, then that challenge becomes even more challenging for us.

So I think consistency is just, consistency in owning the data is really just like my focus here as we start to have these conversations with, you know, our CMO and different teams of why this matters and like how it matters to us.
And to bounce off of that, like even just having raw data as well, so wherever there's opportunity for like a download of the data into a spreadsheet, like things like that is super helpful.
for us, just on an operational level too.

I had a quick question in terms of the first page of this report, just on where the data is pulled from for the, I think it was in terms of the keywords, want to say it might have been on the overall or it might have been on the non-branded.
So these are sessions, English sessions.
And then there was, maybe it was the ranking distribution.
Is all of the data that's keyword based or query based, is this all Google Search Console?
Yeah, exactly.
Okay, and then the same for the non-branded, that's all Google Search Console too.
Yeah, exactly.
So clicks, impressions, and CTRs.
That's why we stitch together like Google Analytics and Google Search Console to give you like, not that we jump between tools, but just we have...
We're leaning in one place.
Okay, fab.
That was great.
Awesome.
We'll take a look at this and add any layers of data that we might need, but this is great for us.
Just with the last five minutes, moving over to the actual, like, what's a priority for this week, next week, looking at that.
Good segue into kind of the five refreshes and kind of what you need from us on the next steps.

So, what I would need to, for you to do is basically take a look at the content OS, or share a link after our meeting, and take, like, go to assignments, considering, and basically review these topics.
These topics are based on the clusters that we suggested, and basically, if you think that topic is relevant, change the status from considering to approve to start.
So, have a batch of net new topics in the backlog, so we can, you know, like, produce these.

On a rolling basis, we are still focusing on content refreshes, so we still have a good batch of topics for the refresh, but at the same time, we want to have this backlog of new topics that we can use going forward.
That's perfect.
this is the key action.
Jump in and look at these as well, as I know you've also added in another strategy page to look at.
But I'm happy to kind of finalize there, and I think that's actually a good point to move on.
just wanted to check.
So I know I raised the question around sort of the pillars and the clusters and getting a little bit more insight there.
And I believe you mentioned you were planning on getting a more detailed breakdown in terms of the keywords, like the keyword search volume beyond just kind of as a holistic number.
But what does the breakdown look like?
And I think for me, one thing would be really important in terms of...
you.

Moving this forward with the content plan and kind of the outline that you've provided, which I think directionally is good, would just be to have, okay, well, what's the breakdown in terms of search volume and for the kind of series as a whole, for each of the series as a whole, and then for each of the kind of content topics, would just be one page in and of itself.

And I think looking at it from that kind of topic perspective, so then we could say, okay, for instance, boards and governance as a huge topic, there's going to be a lot of different sort of keyword focused pillar clusters within that.
And I think you alluded to that in your messages as well.
So just basically having a better understanding of the opportunities that you see from like a series within those from a search volume perspective.
And if something is low search volume, that doesn't necessarily mean that we're
not going to want to target it.
Some of our really key series are low search volume, and that's just the way that it is.
It's just not as much demand, but it's essential for us.
For instance, know, board management, very short series, but really important.

But I think it will really help in terms of being able to give sign off and kind of having that holistic view and better make decisions in terms of, okay, yeah, let's go for this instead of the old one and kind of get that breakdown.
But I think you were kind of alluding to maybe having that as a next step.
Yeah, we already have it.
It's just a matter of like, I'll prepare you a specific view with search volumes and basically divided by the clusters.
So basically, we have this data here for net new topics, the search volume and keyword difficulties separate from the refreshes.
But we have all this data combined.
So basically, I'll send it over today.
Okay, perfect.
Well, then we'll...
Work our way through this.
I've already started to kind of get my eyes on it on Friday, and I'll continue going through.
If I do have, you know, a number of follow-ups that seem more than maybe message-worthy, would we be able to grab any time on Friday to just run through?
Yeah, sure.
We could have this additional meeting, and yeah.
It might not be necessary, but if any questions come up, then I'll ping you, and we can kind of jump on a call just to kind of go through them and just get them done.
Cool.
Yeah, sounds great.
So I'll send you the assignments to, you know, like move from considering to approve to start, and then you will see the search volumes and by cluster, like division by cluster.
So maybe we'll solve it this way.
If not, we can schedule something for Friday.
Perfect.
And then I think in terms of net new content, and probably actually just kind of looking at it as a whole, I think one thing that could also be really useful.

A way of looking at it is thinking about it in terms of some, like I suppose the search topics, so we still can use the various different techniques, like I know obviously we're looking at quick ones with pages that are ranked, like terms that are ranking, you know, number seven, and we can push them up higher, but even if we were to look at it kind of a little bit more holistically in terms of like, right, we're going to cover, say for the next two weeks, this particular topic, say it was on, I don't know, meeting minutes or whatever, we have a lot of different individual pieces that could make up their own blogs that all fit within that series that would all bubble up to your first category, and then that way we can align that with work that we're doing in-house, we can then align it where possible with some of the work that's happening with off-page, and we can, I feel like we might be able to get a little bit more bang for our buck with the internal linking and that kind of pillar cluster strategy as well.

And then obviously there might be a mix of at that point, right, yeah, there's a net new one that we're missing and we can identify that opportunity a little bit more easily or yes, okay, there's this technique that we can use for updating the piece because it's low hanging fruit on page one, but not in the top three, or maybe it's a competitive play and our competitors are winning in the space and we're not, but we can look at it a little bit more realistically and maybe reduce our overlap.

I kind of wanted to throw that out there, know that I was not in some of the earlier calls for the strategy period, which obviously makes it a bit challenging, but I think it could be really useful to kind of see it from like a topic perspective that relates back to the search volume and back to the weeks.

Totally, yeah, like I'll prepare you just like merged views of like a whole backlog of topics and if, yeah, if anyone needs to jump and you can catch as well, so we can wrap up, but talk to you on Slack and I'll send over all the materials.
Well, yeah, thanks for the insights and thanks for today.
No, that's perfect.
And Festus will probably follow up as well.
We've got like a little bit of feedback in terms of some of the content pieces that we've been reviewing.
Really excellent work.
A few extra things that we think that we could add to it.
And we're also working on getting you those.
I actually think Festus has that list that he sent along.
if you do need any more.
No, thanks.
I'm very excited to work in the growth execution stage officially.
So, welcome.
Yes.
And yeah, great to have you.
Great to meet you.
Yeah.
All right.
Thanks, everybody.
Have a good day.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Diligent and GrowthX teams to discuss content strategy, analytics, and next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX transitioning to Phase 2 (execution) with Megan Dickey joining as director supporting strategy](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=80.0)
  - [Analytics dashboard created; team discussing how to better measure content impact on funnel/MQLs](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=596.0)
  - [Content audit complete; team focusing on refreshes and new content aligned with key topics/clusters](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=311.0)
  - [Action needed: Diligent team to review and approve proposed content topics in Content OS](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=1469.0)

## Topics

### Team Introductions and Roles

  - [Megan Dickey: New GrowthX director supporting strategy; background in journalism](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=143.0)
  - [Kezia Farnham: Diligent SEO lead (5 years)](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=190.0)
  - [Festus Shodipo: Diligent SEO team, handles content reviews](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=213.0)
  - [Tyler Phelps: New Diligent search lead (SEO, LLMs, SCM)](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=238.0)
  - [John Habib: Diligent content team lead, long-time employee](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=268.0)

### Content Audit and Technical SEO

  - [404 errors implemented by Festus; broader set being addressed by web team](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=355.0)
  - [Merge/redirect opportunities identified to consolidate content and improve crawl budget](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)
  - [Many high-performing pieces (ranking 1-3) identified to keep as-is](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=421.0)
  - [GrowthX to continue monitoring site health monthly; full audit not needed quarterly](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=500.0)

### Analytics Dashboard and Reporting

  - [Dashboard created with GA4 and GSC data; access granted to key team members](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=529.0)
  - [Includes overall performance, non-branded metrics, conversion events, referral breakdown by LLM](https://fathom.video/share/cyvczqyVRyGKXzYzGmoCGiBn1Qk2Q7Nm?tab=summary&timestamp=567.0)
